refactor_notes: Three concrete improvements against the rubric. (1) Layer boundary (architecture.md §Pattern Proposals): bug creation logic (status='open' assignment) and persistence (db.add/commit) are inline in the route handler; architecture.md explicitly calls for BugService (encapsulate creation + status validation) and BugRepository (data access) so the route layer delegates rather than owns domain and infra concerns. (2) Missing title server-side validation: the happy path calls title.strip() without a prior null/empty guard; a POST with a non-empty description but no title raises AttributeError (500). Architecture.md cross-cutting concerns table lists 'server-side validation (title, description) | BugService + API middleware | Application | AC3'. (3) R2 gap: status is hardcoded 'open' in the route with no recognized-states guard; architecture.md §Risks states 'If BugService or BugRepository doesn't enforce the status enum/allowed values, an invalid status could be stored, violating R2' and calls for both a DB constraint and repository-level validation, neither of which is present.
